metadata.title: Uni[MASK]: Unified Inference In Sequential Decision Problems
organizations: UC Berkeley, Microsoft Research, Carnegie Mellon University

figures.description: Bidirectional Transformer architecture for sequential data

sections.title: Inference Tasks as Masking Schemes
content: Key idea:any inference in sequential decision problemscan be expressed as a sequence masking. The model receives a subset of observed tokens and has to predict some other subset.

sections.title: 1. Can one train on all possible tasks?
content: Training a single model on all tasks (via random masking) not only works, but can do as well or better than specialized models!

sections.title: 2. Which training regime is best for one task?
content: While pre-training on multiple maskings does well, fine-tuning this general model one can improve performance further.

sections.title: 3. Which training regime is best for n tasks?
content: Training on all possible tasks (with random masking) can sometimes enable to do better than training on the n tasks of interest.

sections.title: Results: a single set of model parameters foralltasks
Limitations and Future Work
content: Scale up to more complex environments
Systematically investigate how multi-task performance varies with larger context windows
Train models to roll out trajectories conditioned on other useful properties (e.g., style-matching).

sections.title: Conclusions:
content: The Uni[MASK] framework enables models to:
2. Easily perform multi-task training with no architecture changes
3. Train single models to doall taskswith random-mask pre-training, which perform similarly to specialized models and often surpass them after fine-tuning.
